headline: Gig Economy Workers Lack Protections for 2.5 Million People
context: Estimated 2.5 million Malaysians work in gig economy without EPF SOCSO or minimum wage coverage.

cards.big: 2.5 million gig workers lack EPF SOCSO and minimum wage protections
cards.sub: Grab riders delivery workers and freelancers face zero safety net

cards.big: Average gig worker earns RM2,200 monthly with no employer EPF
cards.sub: Projected retirement savings shortfall of RM180,000 per gig worker

cards.big: 228 gig delivery riders killed in road accidents in 2025
cards.sub: Platform companies provide minimal insurance with RM10,000 death benefit

cards.big: Self-Employment Social Security Act tabled 2024 has not progressed
cards.sub: Platform companies lobbied against mandatory contributions

cards.big: Gig worker vulnerability tests whether labour protections can extend to platform economy
2.5 million gig workers without protections. Labour law built for factories cannot govern platform economies.
